transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: First National, how can I help.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I need to dispute a charge. There's a payment for eighty-five pounds from a company I've never used and I never authorised it.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: DataPrime Solutions. I genuinely don't know who they are.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Let me have a look. Right, I can see that charge. It looks legitimate from our end — the payment went through normally with no flags.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: It doesn't matter if it went through normally, I didn't authorise it. I want to dispute it.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: If the charge went through successfully it means your card was used correctly. Fraudsters can't use your card without the details.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Can you raise a dispute or not?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'd need to send you a paper form for that, which takes up to ten working days to arrive. You can also go into a branch.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's ridiculous. You can't do it on the phone?

planted_violations: failed to verify customer identity before accessing account
refused to raise a formal dispute over the phone, which is against policy
incorrectly stated that a successful transaction cannot be disputed
directed customer to an unnecessarily slow paper process without raising a case
